item.title: 2nd ESO - LC5
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
Writing
Project
[flashcards words="bench:banco,bridge:puente,café:cafetería,lake:lago,open-air theatre:teatro al aire libre,outdoor gym:gimnasio al aire libre,path:camino,picnic area:zona de picnic,playground:patio,skatepark:skatepark,donate:donar,help:ayudar,offer:ofrecer / ofrecerse a,put:poner / depositar,volunteer:ofrecerse voluntari@,find out:descubrir,pick up:recoger,turn on:encender,go away:irse,take part in:formar parte de,work out:entrenar,look after:cuidar de,talk over:hablar sobre,look at:mirar a,turn off:apagar"]

a. Fill the gaps with the following words

bench · bridge · café · lake · open-air theatre · outdoor gym · path · picnic area · playground · skatepark · donate · help · volunteer · turn off · find out

[fill_gaps] 1. We can sit on the [bench] to rest for a while. 2. Let’s cross the river using the [bridge]. 3. I usually drink hot chocolate in the [café]. 4. The [lake] is very big and has ducks swimming in it. 5. Tonight we are going to the [open-air theatre] to watch a play. 6. My dad exercises every morning in the [outdoor gym]. 7. This [path] goes to the forest. 8. Let’s have lunch together in the [picnic area]. 9. The children are playing football in the [playground]. 10. Teenagers like to skate in the [skatepark]. 11. You can [donate] your old clothes to charity. 12. I want to [help] my grandparents with the shopping. 13. She decided to [volunteer] her time to the community. 14. Please [turn off] the TV before going to bed. 15. We are going to [find out] the new museum tomorrow. [/fill_gaps]

Future Tenses

WILL

(+) I will study for the exam tomorrow.
(-): I won't study for the exam tomorrow.
(?): Will you study for the exam tomorrow?

BE GOING TO

(+) I am going to visit my grandparents next week.
(-): I am not going to visit my grandparents next week.
(?): Are you going to visit your grandparents next week?

When do we use 'will' and 'be going to'?

a. Use the correct form of 'will'.

[fill_gaps] 1. I [will call;'ll call] (call) you later. 2. She [won't go;will not go] (not go) to the party. 3. [Will they arrive] (they/arrive) on time? 4. He [will help;'ll help] (help) you with your homework. 5. We [won't be;will not be] (not be) there tomorrow. 6. [Will you visit] (you/visit) your grandparents this weekend? 7. They [will start;'ll start] (start) the meeting at 10 a.m. 8. [Will it rain] (it/rain) today? 9. I [won't forget;will not forget] (not forget) your birthday. 10. [Will she cook] (she/cook) dinner tonight? [/fill_gaps]

b. Use the correct form of 'be going to'

[fill_gaps] 1. I [am going to study; I'm going to study] (study) for the exam tomorrow. 2. She [is going to visit; she's going to visit] (visit) her grandparents next weekend. 3. They [are not going to join; aren't going to join] (not join) us for dinner. 4. [Are you going to watch] (you/watch) the movie tonight? 5. We [are going to travel; 're going to travel] (travel) to Paris next month. 6. He [is not going to participate; isn't going to participate] (not participate) in the competition. 7. [Is she going to help] (she/help) you with your homework? 8. I [am going to start; I'm going to start] (start) a new project at work. 9. We [are going to buy; 're going to buy] (buy) a new car next year. 10. [Are they going to attend] (they/attend) the conference? [/fill_gaps]

c. Use the correct form of 'will' or 'be going to'

[fill_gaps] 1. I'm not really sure, but I think it [will rain] (rain) today. 2. My best friend Ella told me that she [is going to travel] (travel) to France next summer. 3. Hi! Do you need help? I ['ll do; will do] (do) it! 4. We [are going to cook] (cook) dinner together tonight at 20:00. 5. Excuse me, [will you help] (you/help) me cross the street? I'm a little bit old. 6. He can't come to the party because he [is going to watch] (watch) a movie with his sister. 7. It's already June... It [is going to be] (be) really hot soon. 8. I may be wrong, but I believe robots [will rule] (rule) the world in 4050. 9. I have a feeling that today [will be] (be) a good day. 10. They [are going to buy] (buy) a new car soon. John told me this morning. [/fill_gaps]

Write a text about your proposals individually.

  • You can use Word Reference and your notebook ONLY.
  • You have to turn it in at the end of the class.

Include:

  • A title
  • Connectors
  • The LC’s vocabulary
  • will and be going to
  • The relevant information from your Project Builders (problems, solutions)

Structure:

  • Title
  • Paragraph 1
    • Mention the city, some of the city’s problems and which you are going to address
  • Paragraph 2
    • Explain the 5 ideas in your proposal and their advantages (pros) and one disadvantage (con)

Project Builder 1: problems-solutions

There is an empty, abandoned lot in your neighborhood and the mayor wants to transform it. Town hall is accepting proposals to turn it into a useful space for the community. Considering the following problems in your community, you must address 5 proposals in pairs.

Problems in the neighborhood:

  1. Animals
    • There are many stray cats
    • The area is very dirty and full of litter
    • People feed animals on the street and it stinks
    • Dog owners don’t know where to walk their dogs
  2. Culture and leisure
    • The neighbours don’t know each other
    • Elderly people feel lonely
    • Only children don’t have friends to play with
    • There is no library in the neighbourhood
  3. Sports
    • Old people don’t do sport
    • Teenagers spend too many hours at home
    • There is no running track
  4. Science
    • Elderly people don’t know how to use computers
    • Teenagers don’t have a place to build robots
    • Kids don’t know anything about planets, satellites and stars
  5. Ecology
    • There are no places to eat outdoors
    • People want to eat organic fruits and vegetables
    • The city is grey and dark

Project Builder 2: reasons

Create a MOODBOARD with images to use as inspiration for your proposal. You need to include at least 5 park features (dog zone, outdoor gym, etc.) and each one has to include a justification about why you have chosen them. Remember that all the features you choose for your park need to have a justification to benefit the whole community.

  • You can create the moodbord on Canva or Google Slides. Make it visual and attractive!
  • You only need to turn in one moodboard per group.
  • Each person in the group needs to have the five ideas written in their notebook under the title "Project Builder 2".

Project Builder 3: pros-cons

Write at least 3 advantages and 1 disadvantage of each of your ideas from Project Builder 2.

  • Try to be as specific as you can
  • Think about a solution for your disadvantages

Project Builder 4: presentation

  1. The first slide
    • The title
    • Learning Challenge number and name
    • Your full names
    • Your group
  2. Format
    • Minimum 35 font size
    • Easy to read
    • Use Google Slides (or alternatives like Canva or Genially)
  3. Slides
    • Short text, only main ideas
    • 1 slide = 1 main idea
    • Logical order
    • Try to be as formal as possible!
  4. Images
    • One image in each slide
    • Clear images
    • You can use unsplash.com
  5. Colours
    • Use colours!
    • Make it look attractive!
    • You can use Slides Go to get templates
  6. REMEMBER!
    • Include all the information from your Project Builders!

Project Builder 5

Write a script of your presentation considering the following:

  • Think about your audience and how formal you want the language in your speech to be.
  • Choose a suitable phrase to get everyone’s attention at the beginning.
  • Introduce yourself and say why you are making the speech.
  • Use the pronoun you to talk directly to the audience.
  • Use lots of positive adjectives.

item.title: 4th ESO - LC5
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
[flashcards words = "lawyer:abogado,thief:ladrón,judge:juez,guilty:culpable,capture:atrapar / capturar,break into:allanar,cause of death:causa de la muerte,case:caso,arrow:flecha,escape:escapar,victim:víctima,life sentence:cadena perpetua,defendant:acusado,guns:armas (de fuego),experts:expertos,innocent:inocente,knife:cuchillo,jury:jurado,punish:castigar,murderer:asesino,alibi:coartada,murder:asesinato,robbery:robo (con violencia),burglar:ladrón (de casas),rape:violación,crime scene:escena del crimen,uncover:destapar,evidence:prueba,arson:incendio provocado,court:juzgado,jail:cárcel,forensic scientist:científico forense,to witness:presenciar,a witness:testigo,hijack:secuestrar (vehículo / avión),fingerprints:huellas dactilares,blackmail:chantaje,to kidnap:secuestrar,rapist:violador,criminal:criminal,theft:robo (en general),burglary:robo (en una casa),mugging:atraco (ataque a alguien con intención de robar),shoplifting:robar en una tienda,footprint:huella / pisada (de pie o zapato),suspect:sospechoso,clue:pista,vandalism:vandalismo,forgery:falsificación,pickpocket:carterista / hurto (del bolsillo de alguien) "]

Grammar
Relative Pronouns

[tabla] Relative Pronoun | Use | Example .A that / which /A | objects | The computer .A that /A I use to work is brand new.
This pen, .A which /A is extremely cheap, is really good. .A where /A | places | The place .A where /A John lives is called Bergen. .A when /A | moments (time) | The day .A when /A the crime was comitted was cloudy. .A why /A | reason | The reason .A why /A John lives in Canada is because he loves it. .A who /A | people (direct) | John, .A who /A is my neighbour, always wakes up early. .A whom /A | people (indirect) | The friend .A whom /A I called yesterday was not at home. .A whose /A | possession | May, .A whose /A sister is a musician, plays the piano really well. .A how /A | the way we do things | Practicing a lot is .A how /A I finally got my driving license. [/tabla]

a. Choose the correct relative pronoun

[fill_gaps] 1. The man [whom] I called didn’t answer. 2. She has a friend [who] lives in Canada. 3. That’s the car [that;which] broke down yesterday. 4. I don’t like people [who] are rude. 5. The book [that;which] you gave me was excellent. 6. I know a hotel [that;which] has many rooms available. 7. There was a time [when] humans slept in caves. 8. This is the house [where] I would like to live. [/fill_gaps]

b. Join the two sentences using the correct relative pronoun

[fill_gaps] 1. The woman is my teacher. She lives next door. [The woman who lives next door is my teacher;the woman who is my teacher lives next door;The woman which lives next door is my teacher;the woman which is my teacher lives next door] 2. This horror movie is called Dracula. I watched it last Saturday. [The horror movie that I watched last Saturday is called Dracula;The horror movie which I watched last Saturday is called Dracula] 3. This house is warm. I have lived in this house my whole life. [The house where I have lived my whole life is warm] 4. My sister is a good person. I admire her because of that. [The reason why I admire my sister is because she is a good person] 5. This is the girl. She has a brother called Matt. [This is the girl whose brother is called Matt] 6. Christmas is my favourite time of the year. I was born then. [Christmas, when I was born, is my favourite time of the year] [/fill_gaps]

item.title: About
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
English & Chill was born with the purpose of helping students review their English contents.

Here, you'll find:

· Vocabulary flashcards
· Grammar explanations
· Readings
· Listenings
· Writing outlines
· Exam simulations

Enjoy!

1-15

[fill_gaps] 1. be - [was] / [were] - [been] - [ser; estar] / [ser; estar] 2. beat - [beat] - [beaten] - [golpear; pegar] 3. become - [became] - [become] - [convertirse; transformarse] 4. begin - [began] - [begun] - [empezar; comenzar] 5. bite - [bit] - [bitten] - [morder] 6. blow - [blew] - [blown] - [soplar] 7. break - [broke] - [broken] - [romper] 8. bring - [brought] - [brought] - [llevar] 9. burn - [burnt; burned] - [burnt; burned] - [quemar] 10. build - [built] - [built] - [construir] 11. buy - [bought] - [bought] - [comprar] 12. can - [could] - [been able to] - [poder] 13. catch - [caught] - [caught] - [atrapar; coger] 14. choose - [chose] - [chosen] - [elegir; escoger] 15. come - [came] - [come] - [venir] [/fill_gaps]

16-30

[fill_gaps] 16. cost - [cost] - [cost] - [costar] 17. cut - [cut] - [cut] - [cortar] 18. do - [did] - [done] - [hacer] 19. draw - [drew] - [drawn] - [dibujar] 20. dream - [dreamt; dreamed] - [dreamt; dreamed] - [soñar] 21. drink - [drank] - [drunk] - [beber] 22. drive - [drove] - [driven] - [conducir] 23. eat - [ate] - [eaten] - [comer] 24. fall - [fell] - [fallen] - [caer; caerse] 25. feed - [fed] - [fed] - [alimentar; dar de comer] 26. feel - [felt] - [felt] - [sentir] 27. fight - [fought] - [fought] - [luchar] 28. find - [found] - [found] - [encontrar] 29. fly - [flew] - [flown] - [volar] 30. forget - [forgot] - [forgotten] - [olvidar] [/fill_gaps]

31-45

[fill_gaps] 31. forgive - [forgave] - [forgiven] - [perdonar] 32. freeze - [froze] - [frozen] - [congelar; congelarse] 33. get - [got] - [got; gotten] - [conseguir; alcanzar; llegar] 34. give - [gave] - [given] - [dar] 35. go - [went] - [gone] - [ir] 36. grow - [grew] - [grown] - [crecer; cultivar] 37. hang - [hung] - [hung] - [colgar] 38. have - [had] - [had] - [tener] 39. hear - [heard] - [heard] - [oír] 40. hide - [hid] - [hidden] - [esconder; esconderse] 41. hit - [hit] - [hit] - [golpear; pegar] 42. hold - [held] - [held] - [sostener; mantener; aguantar] 43. hurt - [hurt] - [hurt] - [herir; hacer daño] 44. keep - [kept] - [kept] - [guardar; mantener; permanecer] 45. know - [knew] - [known] - [saber; conocer] [/fill_gaps]

46-60

[fill_gaps] 46. lead - [led] - [led] - [llevar a; conducir a] 47. learn - [learnt; learned] - [learnt; learned] - [aprender] 48. leave - [left] - [left] - [dejar; abandonar; salir] 49. lend - [lent] - [lent] - [prestar; dejar] 50. let - [let] - [let] - [dejar; permitir; dar permiso] 51. lie - [lay] - [lain] - [tumbarse; tumbar] 52. lose - [lost] - [lost] - [perder] 53. make - [made] - [made] - [hacer; preparar] 54. meet - [met] - [met] - [conocer; reunirse; quedar] 55. pay - [paid] - [paid] - [pagar] 56. put - [put] - [put] - [poner] 57. read - [read] - [read] - [leer] 58. ride - [rode] - [ridden] - [montar; cabalgar] 59. ring - [rang] - [rung] - [sonar] 60. run - [ran] - [run] - [correr] [/fill_gaps]

61-75

[fill_gaps] 61. say - [said] - [said] - [decir] 62. see - [saw] - [seen] - [ver] 63. sell - [sold] - [sold] - [vender] 64. send - [sent] - [sent] - [enviar] 65. set - [set] - [set] - [establecer] 66. shake - [shook] - [shaken] - [sacudir; remover; barajar] 67. shine - [shone] - [shone] - [brillar] 68. show - [showed] - [shown; showed] - [enseñar] 69. shut - [shut] - [shut] - [cerrar] 70. sing - [sang] - [sung] - [cantar] 71. sit - [sat] - [sat] - [sentarse] 72. sleep - [slept] - [slept] - [dormir] 73. speak - [spoke] - [spoken] - [hablar] 74. spell - [spelt; spelled] - [spelt; spelled] - [deletrear] 75. spend - [spent] - [spent] - [gastar; invertir; utilizar] [/fill_gaps]

76-88

[fill_gaps] 76. stand - [stood] - [stood] - [aguantar] 77. steal - [stole] - [stolen] - [robar] 78. swim - [swam] - [swum] - [nadar] 79. take - [took] - [taken] - [coger; tomar] 80. teach - [taught] - [taught] - [enseñar] 81. tell - [told] - [told] - [decir; contar] 82. think - [thought] - [thought] - [pensar] 83. throw - [threw] - [thrown] - [lanzar] 84. understand - [understood] - [understood] - [entender; comprender] 85. wake - [woke] - [woken] - [despertar] 86. wear - [wore] - [worn] - [llevar; llevar puesto] 87. win - [won] - [won] - [ganar] 88. write - [wrote] - [written] - [escribir] [/fill_gaps]

item.title: 2nd ESO - LC1
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
[fancy-button] text= Extra Grammar EXS! link= https://englishandchill.com/2nd-eso/2nd-lc1/lc1-extra-practice-past-simple/ color= #560319 [/fancy-button]
[fancy-button] text= Writing link= #writing [/fancy-button]
[fancy-button] text= Project link= #project [/fancy-button]

Time Capsule

[flashcards words="close:cerrar, container:contenedor, hide:esconder, include:incluir, inside:dentro, items:objetos / elementos, outside:fuera, safe:seguro"]

Verbs

[flashcards words="buy:comprar, sell:vender, win:ganar, save (money):ahorrar, agree:estar de acuerdo, disagree:estar en desacuerdo / discrepar, appear:aparecer, disappear:desaparecer, connect:conectar, disconnect:desconectar, lend:prestar, borrow:tomar prestado, lose:perder, send:enviar, receive:recibir, spend:invertir / gastar"]

Feelings

[flashcards words="annoyed:molesto, bored:aburrido, embarrassed:avergonzado, excited:emocionado, relaxed:relajado, scared:asustado, surprised:sorprendido, tired:cansado, unhappy:descontento, worried:preocupado"]

a. Fill the gaps with words from the Vocabulary section.

[fill_gaps] 1. I want to [buy] a new phone next week. 2. I [agree;disagree] with your opinion about the movie. 3. My keys always [disappear] when I need them. 4. Please [disconnect] the cable before you leave. 5. Can you [lend] me your book for a few days? 6. I always bring my umbrella when I go [outside]. 7. I will [send] the email this afternoon. 8. I don’t like to [spend] money on things I don’t need. 9. She looked very [annoyed;unhappy] after waiting for an hour. 10. I'm very [bored] when there is nothing to do. 11. He feels [embarassed] when he falls in front of everyone. 12. I’m so [excited] about my birthday party! 13. After a massage, I am always completely [relaxed]. 14. I am often [scared] when I watch horror movies. 15. You look very [surprised]! Sorry for coming without calling before. 16. After working all day, I’m always [tired]. 17. She seems [worried;annoyed;unhappy] because her project isn't working. 18. He’s very [worried] about the exam results. [/fill_gaps]

Past Simple

[tabla] PAST SIMPLE (TO BE) .A.b + /b /A | .R .b - /b /R | .V .b ? /b /V I, He, She, It + .A was /A | I, He, She, It + .R wasn't /R | .V Was /V + I, he, she, it? We, You, They + .A were /A | We, You, They + .R weren't /R | .V Were /V + we, you, they? [/tabla] [tabla] He .A was /A at home. | He .R wasn't /R at home. | .V Was /V he at home? They .A were /A happy | They .R weren't /R happy. | .V Were /V they happy? [/tabla] [tabla] PAST SIMPLE (ACTIONS) Regular Verbs .A .b + /b /A | .R .b - /b /R | .V .b ? /b /V Subject .A + base verb + ed /A | Subject + .R didn't /R + base verb | .V Did /V + subject + base verb? [/tabla] [tabla] She .A watched /A TV. | She .R didn't watch /R TV. | .V Did /V she watch TV? [/tabla] [tabla] Irregular Verbs .A .b + /b /A | .R .b - /b /R | .V .b ? /b /V Subject .A + base verb (2nd column) /A | Subject + .R didn't /R + base verb | .V Did /V + subject + base verb? [/tabla] [tabla] She .A slept /A all day. | She .R didn't sleep /R all day. | .V Did /V she sleep all day? [/tabla]

There was / There were

[tabla] There was / There were Countable nouns (singular) .A .b + /b /A | .R .b - /b /R | .V .b ? /b /V .A There was /A a tree. | .R There wasn't /R any tree. | .V Was there /V any tree? Countable nouns (plural) .A .b + /b /A | .R .b - /b /R | .V .b ? /b /V .A There were /A two trees. | .R There weren't /R any trees. | .V Were there /V any trees? Uncountable nouns .A .b + /b /A | .R .b - /b /R | .V .b ? /b /V .A There was /A money on the table. | .R There wasn't /R any money on the table. | .V Was there /V any money on the table? [/tabla]

a. Fill the gaps with the Past Simple form of the verb To Be.

[fill_gaps]
1. He [was] (be) nice to you yesterday.
2. We [weren't;were not] (not be) cold.
3. [Was] (be) he a footballer?
4. Where [was] (be) John?
5. We [were] (be) students once.
6. He [wasn't;was not] (not be) a journalist.
7. We [weren't;were not] (not be) at home.
8. [Were] (be) you a nice student?
9. Where [were] (be) David and Leo?
10. How [was] (be) your mum?
11. I [was] (be) a professional guitarist 20 years ago.
12. I [wasn't;was not] (not be) happy yesterday.
13. [Was] (be) I your best friend when we were 7?
[/fill_gaps]

b. Fill the gaps with the Past Simple form of the following verbs.

[fill_gaps]
1. He [drove] (drive) to work yesterday.
2. I [didn't think;did not think] (not think) you were right.
3. [Did we have] (we/have) enough time?
4. I [ate] (eat) cereal yesterday morning.
5. [Did she travel] (she/travel) a lot?
6. You [watched] (watch) a lot of TV.
7. He [didn't read;did not read] (not read) the newspaper.
8. [Did she dance] (she/dance) often?
9. What [did you do] (you/do) last weekend?
10. Michael [didn't drink;did not drink] (not drink) much tea.
[/fill_gaps]

1. Fill the gaps with the correct form of There was / There were

[fill_gaps] 1. [There was] (be) a big party at my house last weekend. 2. [There weren't;There were not] (not be) many people at the concert. 3. [Was there] (be) a restaurant near your hotel? 4. [There were] (be) two cats in the garden yesterday. 5. [There wasn't;There was not] (not be) any milk in the fridge. 6. [Were there] (be) any students in the library? 7. [There was] (be) a beautiful rainbow after the storm. 8. [There weren't;There were not] (not be) any buses after 10 p.m. 9. [Was there] (be) a good film on TV last night? 10. [There were] (be) lots of tourists at the museum. [/fill_gaps]

Informal email: 'a fun day out'

[tabla] INFORMAL EMAIL: 'A FUN DAY OUT' Structure | Example Greeting | Hi Jane! Opening | How are you? I hope you’re well. I want to tell you about last Saturday… it was amazing! It was one of the best days I can remember. Body (paragraph 1 + paragraph 2 + question) | Last Saturday, I went on a trip with my family to a beautiful village in Mallorca called Valldemossa. The village is in the mountains and it’s very quiet and beautiful. The houses are old and the streets are small and nice.

We walked around the village, visited a museum and ate delicious Mallorcan food. I took lots of photos! The weather was sunny and warm, so it was perfect.

I really loved the trip. Have you ever been to Valldemossa? Closing | Write soon! Goodbye | Best wishes, Signature | Sofía [/tabla]

Structure examples

[tabla] Greetings Hi Hello Hey Hey there! Dear (name) Opening How are you? I hope you're doing well. I wanted to tell you about something fun I did recently. Guess what? I had a great weekend! Closing Best wishes, Bye for now, See you, Signature (Your name) [/tabla]

Project: 'A Time Capsule'

Project Builder 1

Middle Ages · Roman Empire (link 1 & link 2) · Ancient India · Native Americans (link 1, link 2 & link 3) · Victorian London · 13 Colonies (USA)

Research your time era with the links. Summarize the main ideas: 

  • Time period and country
  • Clothes
  • Food
  • Entertainment
  • Technology

Project Builder 2

Choose what objects to include in your virtual time capsule. You need at least one for each category in PB1.

Project Builder 3

Write your script (what you are going to say) and create your presentation.

How to prepare your presentation?

⚠️ IMPORTANT: you can't include text (paragraphs) or long sentences in your presentation, only keywords. You can use images and other visual resources.

  • 1 slide to introduce yourselves
    • name of the group, names of the members, time era)
  • 1 slide (at least) for each category
    • Time period and country
    • Clothes
    • Food
    • Entertainment
    • Technology
  • 1 slide with your objects for the Time Capsule
  • 1 slide to finish the presentation
    • 'Thank you for your attention', 'thank you for listening', etc.

⏳ Each member is going to speak for, at least, 1 minute (apart from the intro and outro).

Final Project

Perform your oral presentation with your group.

item.title: 2nd ESO: Final Review
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
<
[flashcards words="bench:banco,bridge:puente,café:cafetería,lake:lago,open-air theatre:teatro al aire libre,outdoor gym:gimnasio al aire libre,path:camino,picnic area:zona de picnic,playground:patio,skatepark:skatepark,donate:donar,help:ayudar,offer:ofrecer / ofrecerse a,put:poner / depositar,volunteer:ofrecerse voluntari@,find out:descubrir,pick up:recoger,turn on:encender,go away:irse,take part in:formar parte de,work out:entrenar,look after:cuidar de,talk over:hablar sobre,look at:mirar a,turn off:apagar"]

a. Fill the gaps with the following words:

bench · lake · café · open-air theatre · picnic area · skatepark · help · pick up · volunteer · take part in · look after · turn off

[fill_gaps] 1. We sat on the [bench] and watched the people walk by. 2. He loves swimming in the [lake] during summer. 3. Let’s meet at the [café] for a chat. 4. They watched a Shakespeare play at the [open-air theatre]. 5. We had lunch in the [picnic area] near the river. 6. He practiced new tricks at the [skatepark]. 7. Can you [help] me carry these bags, please? 8. I forgot to [pick up] my jacket from your house. 9. She wants to [volunteer] on weekends at the pet shelter. 10. We will [take part in] the school’s charity run. 11. Who will [look after] the baby while you’re away? 12. Please [turn off] the TV before going to sleep. [/fill_gaps]

b. Fill the gaps with the following words:

playground · outdoor gym · bridge · path · donate · work out · offer · go away · put · find out · talk over · turn on · look at

[fill_gaps] 1. The children played happily in the [playground]. 2. He goes to the [outdoor gym] every morning. 3. We crossed a beautiful old [bridge] on our walk. 4. Follow the forest [path] to the campsite. 5. We plan to [donate] old books to the library. 6. She tries to [work out] three times a week. She wants to be fit. 7. I should [offer] my seat to elderly people. 8. Please just [go away] and let me concentrate. 9. [Put] your phone in your bag during class, please. 10. I want to [find out] what really happened. 11. Let’s [talk over] your problems, one by one. 12. Could you [turn on] the lamp? It’s too dark in here. 13. She stopped to [look at] the painting in the gallery. [/fill_gaps]

a. Use the correct form of 'will' or 'be going to'

[fill_gaps] 1. I'm not really sure, but I think it [will rain] (rain) today. 2. My best friend Ella told me that she [is going to travel] (travel) to France next summer. 3. Hi! Do you need help? I ['ll do; will do] (do) it! 4. We [are going to cook] (cook) dinner together tonight at 20:00. 5. Excuse me, [will you help] (you/help) me cross the street? I'm a little bit old. 6. He can't come to the party because he [is going to watch] (watch) a movie with his sister. 7. It's already June... It [is going to be] (be) really hot soon. 8. I may be wrong, but I believe robots [will rule] (rule) the world in 4050. 9. I have a feeling that today [will be] (be) a good day. 10. They [are going to buy] (buy) a new car soon. John told me this morning. [/fill_gaps]

b. Use the correct form of Past Simple or Past Continuous

[fill_gaps] 1. I [was walking] (walk) to school when I [saw] (see) an accident. 2. She [was reading] (read) a book while he [was watching] (watch) TV. 3. They [didn't do] (not do) their homework. 4. The lights [went] (go) out while we [were having] (have) dinner. 5. My parents [didn't go] (not/go) to the party last night. 6. It [was raining] (rain) when we [left] (leave) the house. 7. What [were you doing] (you/do) yesterday all morning? 8. He [broke] (break) his arm while he [was skateboarding] (skateboard). 9. We [were studying] (study) for the exam when the teacher [arrived] (arrive). 10. She [called] (call) me while I [was leaving] (leave) the office. [/fill_gaps]

1-10

[fill_gaps] 1. be - [was] / [were] - [ser; estar] / [ser; estar] 2. beat - [beat] - [golpear; pegar] 3. become - [became] - [convertirse; transformarse] 4. begin - [began] - [empezar; comenzar] 5. bite - [bit] - [morder] 6. blow - [blew] - [soplar] 7. break - [broke] - [romper] 8. bring - [brought] - [llevar] 9. burn - [burnt; burned] - [quemar] 10. build - [built] - [construir] [/fill_gaps]

11-20

[fill_gaps] 11. buy - [bought] - [comprar] 12. can - [could] - [poder] 13. catch - [caught] - [atrapar; coger] 14. choose - [chose] - [elegir; escoger] 15. come - [came] - [venir] 16. cost - [cost] - [costar] 17. cut - [cut] - [cortar] 18. do - [did] - [hacer] 19. draw - [drew] - [dibujar] 20. dream - [dreamt; dreamed] - [soñar] [/fill_gaps]

21-30

[fill_gaps] 21. drink - [drank] - [beber] 22. drive - [drove] - [conducir] 23. eat - [ate] - [comer] 24. fall - [fell] - [caer; caerse] 25. feed - [fed] - [alimentar; dar de comer] 26. feel - [felt] - [sentir] 27. fight - [fought] - [luchar] 28. find - [found] - [encontrar] 29. fly - [flew] - [volar] 30. forget - [forgot] - [olvidar] [/fill_gaps]

31-40

[fill_gaps] 31. forgive - [forgave] - [perdonar] 32. freeze - [froze] - [congelar; congelarse] 33. get - [got] - [conseguir; alcanzar; llegar] 34. give - [gave] - [dar] 35. go - [went] - [ir] 36. grow - [grew] - [crecer; cultivar] 37. hang - [hung] - [colgar] 38. have - [had] - [tener] 39. hear - [heard] - [oír] 40. hide - [hid] - [esconder; esconderse] [/fill_gaps]

41-50

[fill_gaps] 41. hit - [hit] - [golpear; pegar] 42. hold - [held] - [sostener; mantener; aguantar] 43. hurt - [hurt] - [herir; hacer daño] 44. keep - [kept] - [guardar; mantener; permanecer] 45. know - [knew] - [saber; conocer] 46. lead - [led] - [llevar a; conducir a] 47. learn - [learnt; learned] - [aprender] 48. leave - [left] - [dejar; abandonar; salir] 49. lend - [lent] - [prestar; dejar] 50. let - [let] - [dejar; permitir; dar permiso] [/fill_gaps]

51-60

[fill_gaps] 51. lie - [lay] - [tumbarse; tumbar] 52. lose - [lost] - [perder] 53. make - [made] - [hacer; preparar] 54. meet - [met] - [conocer; reunirse; quedar] 55. pay - [paid] - [pagar] 56. put - [put] - [poner] 57. read - [read] - [leer] 58. ride - [rode] - [montar; cabalgar] 59. ring - [rang] - [sonar] 60. run - [ran] - [correr] [/fill_gaps]

61-70

[fill_gaps] 61. say - [said] - [decir] 62. see - [saw] - [ver] 63. sell - [sold] - [vender] 64. send - [sent] - [enviar] 65. set - [set] - [establecer] 66. shake - [shook] - [sacudir; remover; barajar] 67. shine - [shone] - [brillar] 68. show - [showed] - [enseñar] 69. shut - [shut] - [cerrar] 70. sing - [sang] - [cantar] [/fill_gaps]

71-80

[fill_gaps] 71. sit - [sat] - [sentarse] 72. sleep - [slept] - [dormir] 73. speak - [spoke] - [hablar] 74. spell - [spelt,spelled] - [deletrear] 75. spend - [spent] - [gastar,invertir,utilizar] 76. stand - [stood] - [aguantar] 77. steal - [stole] - [robar] 78. swim - [swam] - [nadar] 79. take - [took] - [coger,tomar] 80. teach - [taught] - [enseñar] [/fill_gaps]

81-88

[fill_gaps] 81. tell - [told] - [decir; contar] 82. think - [thought] - [pensar] 83. throw - [threw] - [lanzar] 84. understand - [understood] - [entender; comprender] 85. wake - [woke] - [despertar] 86. wear - [wore] - [llevar; llevar puesto] 87. win - [won] - [ganar] 88. write - [wrote] - [escribir] [/fill_gaps]

item.title: 2nd ESO - LC2
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
Arts & Crafts

[flashcards words= "street art:arte callejero, illustration:ilustración, design:diseño / diseñar, music:música, scuplture:escultura, photography:fotografía, dance:baile / bailar, painting:pintura / cuadro"]

Artistic Adjectives

[flashcards words= "incredible:increíble, huge:enorme, enormous:enorme, colorful:colorido / lleno de color, pretty:bonito, attractive:atractivo / atractivo, strange:extraño / raro, awful:horrible, terrible:horrible, bright:brillante (luminoso), brilliant:brillante (ocurrente), unusual:inusual / raro"]

Verbs

[flashcards words= "shine:brillar, sit:sentarse, take (photos):hacer (fotos), watch:mirar / observar, work:trabajar, walk:caminar, paint:pintar"]

Mona Lisa - The Scream

[flashcards words= "coat:abrigo, crime:crimen, famous:famoso, museum:museo, mysterious:misterioso, police:policía, blood red:rojo sangre, experience:experiencia / experimentar, frightened:aterrorizado, ill:enfermo,loud:fuerte (sonido / ruido), scary:escalofriante / alarmante, scream:grito / gritar, sky:cielo, sunset:atardecer / puesta de sol"]

Materials

[flashcards words= "cardboard:cartón, paper:papel, cotton:algodón, plastic:plástico, glass:cristal / vidrio, wood:madera, leather:cuero, wool:lana, metal:metal"]

Feelings

[flashcards words= "joy:alegría, fear:miedo, surprise:sorpresa, sadness:tristeza, anger:ira / rabia, disgust:repulsión / asco"]

a. Fill the gaps with the following words

incredible · huge · colorful · pretty · strange · awful · attractive · bright · brilliant · enormous · unusual · paint · shine · sit · take (photos) · watch · work · coat · crime · museum

[fill_gaps] 1. The view from the top of the mountain was [incredible]! 2. That building is so [huge;enormous]! It looks like a castle! 3. I love the [pretty;colorful] flowers in the garden. 4. She is wearing a [pretty;colorful] dress for the party. I love it! 5. The man told me a [strange;unusual] story about a ghost in his house. 6. The food in that restaurant was [awful], so I didn't finish it. 7. The actor is very [pretty;attractive] and popular. 8. The sun is very [bright] this morning. 9. That was a [brilliant] idea! 10. They live in an [huge;enormous] house near the beach. 11. It was an [strange;unusual] day, because it snowed in summer. 12. Children like to [paint] pictures of animals. 13. The stars [shine] in the night sky. 14. Please [sit] next to me. 15. Tourists like to [take] photos in front of monuments. 16. Let's [watch] a movie tonight. 17. Many people [work] in this company. 18. In winter you need a warm [coat]. 19. The police are investigating a [crime]. 20. We visited an art [museum] last weekend. [/fill_gaps]

Past Continuous

[tabla] (REMINDER) I/He/She/It | was We/You/They | were [/tabla] [tabla] STRUCTURE .A .b Affirmative /b /A | Subject + .A was/were /A + verb (-ing) .R .b Negative /b /R | Subject + .R wasn't/weren't /R + verb (-ing) .V .b Interrogative /b /V | .V Was/Were /V + subject + verb (-ing)? [/tabla] [tabla] EXAMPLES I .A was sleeping. /A | I .R wasn't sleeping. /R |.V Was /V I .V sleeping? /V You .A were sleeping. /A | You .R weren't sleeping. /R |.V Were /V You .V sleeping? /V [/tabla]

Past Continuous & Past Simple

[tabla] PAST SIMPLE & PAST CONTINUOUS Main Structure .b.YPC/Y/b+ .Lwhen/L + .b.APS/A/b|I .Ywas driving/Y .Lwhen/L my phone .Arang/A. .b.APS/A/b + .Lwhile/L + .b.YPC/Y/b|I .Nfell/N .Lwhile/L I .Ywas dancing/Y [/tabla] [tabla] Inversions & Variations .Lwhen/L + .b.APS/A/b, .b.YPC/Y/b|.LWhen/L my phone .Arang/A, I .Ywas driving/Y. .Lwhile/L + .b.YPC/Y/b, .b.APS/A/b|.LWhile/L I .Ywas dancing/Y, I .Afell/A. .Lwhile/L + .b.YPC/Y/b, .b.YPC/Y/b|.LWhile/L I .Ywas studying, my sister /Ywas watching TV.Y/Y. [/tabla]

a. Choose the correct form of Past Continuous

[fill_gaps] 1. John [was walking] (walk) in the forest all morning. 2. They [weren't eating;were not eating] (not eat) dinner when I arrived. 3. She [was reading] (read) a book yesterday afternoon. 4. The children [were playing] (play) in the garden. 5. I [wasn't sleeping;was not sleeping] (not sleep) when the phone rang. 6. We [weren't cooking;were not cooking] (not cook) at that time. 7. He [was watching] (watch) TV while his brother was playing outside. 8. [Were you talking] (you/talk) to your friend when I called? 9. [Was she running] (she/run) in the park at 6 p.m.? 10. [Were they studying] (they/study) all night for the exam? [/fill_gaps]

b. Choose the correct form of Past Continuous or Past Simple

[fill_gaps] 1. Helen [was sleeping] (sleep) when I came home. 2. I [wasn't watching;was not watching] (not watch) TV when the phone rang. 3. [Did the vase break] (the vase/break) while you were cleaning the shelf? 4. She [was reading] (read) a book when her friend called. 5. We [weren't playing;were not playing] (not play) in the park when it started raining. 6. I [was cooking] (cook) dinner when my brother arrived. 7. [Was he talking] (he/talk) on the phone when you knocked on the door? 8. They [arrived] (arrive) while we were having lunch. 9. My sister [wasn't sleeping;was not sleeping] (not sleep) when the alarm went off. 10. [Were you reading] (you/read) a magazine when the lights went out? [/fill_gaps]

c. Choose the correct form of Past Continuous or Past Simple

[fill_gaps] 1. While I [was studying] (study), the electricity [went] (go) out. 2. The students [were talking] (talk) while the teacher [was writing] (write) on the board. 3. When the fire alarm [rang] (ring), we [were having] (have) a meeting. 4. While they [were waiting] (wait) for the bus, it [started] (start) to snow. 5. I [wasn't listening;was not listening] (not listen) when the boss [gave] (give) the instructions. 6. While my mom [was driving] (drive), she [saw] (see) a beautiful rainbow. 7. [Was it raining] (it / rain) while you [were walking] (walk) to the gym? 8. We [found] (find) some old photos while we [were tidying] (tidy) the attic. 9. When you [called] (call) me, I [wasn't doing;was not doing] (not do) anything important. 10. While the chef [was preparing] (prepare) the soup, the assistants [were chopping] (chop) the vegetables. [/fill_gaps]

Informal email

Hi Luis,

How are you? I want to tell you about a marvelous book I just read. It's called Extraordinary Women. I think it's amazing. The characters are very well developed, and the plot twists surprised me every single time.

What's your favourite TV show? Why do you like it? Tell me about it!

See you soon,

John

[tabla] Structure | Example .b Greeting /b | Dear John, .b Opening /b | It's nice to hear from you, and I'm happy to answer to your questions. .b Main paragraph 1 /b | My favourite show is called 'Cooking Challenge'. I must say it's a great show. In this programme, there are some contestants and they must cook a meal for the judges. Then, the judges taste the dishes and decide which is the best. The winner receives a prize of 500€. .b Main paragraph 2 /b | I like it because, as you know, I love cooking, so with this programme I can learn how to prepare food in different ways. However, some recipes are quite difficult to learn. .b Closing /b | Well, I have to go now. Let me know about your favourite show, too. .b Goodbye /b | Best wishes, .b Signature /b | Luis [/tabla] [tabla] Structure examples Greetings Hi / Hello / Hey / Hey there! / Dear (name) Openings How are you? I hope you're doing well. I wanted to tell you about something fun I did recently. Guess what? I had a great weekend! Closings Best wishes, Bye for now, See you, Signature (your name) [/tabla]

Project Builder 1

Design an art gallery on a separate sheet of paper. Choose a name and design a map and some features for your gallery.

Project Builder 2: 'Street Art'

Take a picture of street art (it must be outside the school). Upload it to the task. Describe it:

  • where did you find it?
  • what does it evoke?
  • how does it make you feel?

Project Builder 3: 'Group Portrait'

You will take a picture with your group members. All together, you must represent a feeling (you will have one assigned). Upload it to the task. Once you finish, you're going to describe the picture you took using Past Continuous.

Project Builder 4: 'Mystery Object'

At home, you will take two pictures of an object. In the first one, you will only show a little piece of that object. In the second one, you will show it entirely. Upload those pictures to the task.

Then, on your notebook, you're going to describe the object (material, purpose, etc.). Then your classmates need to guess which object you chose.

item.title: 2nd ESO: LC3
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded: All you need to know about the book will be explained in class. Shall you have any questions, ask your teachers.

item.title: 2nd ESO- LC0
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
Present Simple

[tabla] STRUCTURE TO BE .A .b + /b /A | .R .b - /b /R | .V .b ? /b /V I .A am /A | I .R am not /R | .V Am /V I? You, we, they .A are /A | You, we, they .R aren't /R | .V Are /V we, you, they? He, she, it .A is/A | He, she, it .R isn't /R | .V Is /V he, she, it? [/tabla] [tabla] EXAMPLES I .A am /A your doctor. | She .R isn't /R my friend. | .V Are /V you a good person? [/tabla] [tabla] ACTIONS .A .b + /b /A | .R .b - /b /R | .V .b ? /b /V I, we, you, they .A play /A | I, we, you, they .R don't play /R | .V Do /V I, we, you, they .V play/V? He, she, it .A plays /A | He, she, it .R doesn't play /R | .V Does /V he, she, it .V play/V? [/tabla] [tabla] I .A swim /A every morning. | He .R doesn't speak /R English every day. | .V Do /V they usually .V dance/V? [/tabla]

KEYWORDS

[flashcards words="always:siempre,usually:normalmente,often:a menudo,rarely:rara vez,hardly ever:casi nunca,never:nunca,every day:cada día,once a week:una vez a la semana,twice a week:dos veces por semana"]

Present Continuous

[tabla] STRUCTURE .A .b + /b /A | .R .b - /b /R | .V .b ? /b /V I .A am playing /A | I .R am not playing/R | .V Am /V I .V playing/V? You, we, they .A are playing /A | You, we, they .R aren't playing /R | .V Are /V we, you, they .V playing/V? He, she, it .A is playing /A | He, she, it .R isn't playing /R | .V Is /V he, she, it .V playing/V? [/tabla]

KEYWORDS

[flashcards words="now:ahora,right now:ahora mismo,at the moment:en este momento,at present:por ahora"]

Question Words

[flashcards words="what:qué,which:cuál/qué,where:dónde,when:cuándo,why:por qué,how:cuándo"] [tabla] How to use | Example What (objects/ideas) | .i What is that?
What are you doing? /i Which (objects/ideas) | .i Which book do you prefer?
Which is your favourite book? /i Where (places) | .i Where do you live? /i When (time) | .i When are you leaving? /i Why (reason) | .i Why are you so rude? /i How (manner) | .i How can I build a birdhouse? /i [/tabla]

Possessives

[tabla] Subject Pronouns | Object Pronouns | Possessive Adjectives | Possessive Pronouns I | Me | My | Mine You | You | Your | Yours He | Him | His | His She | Her | Her | Hers It | It | Its | Its We | Us | Our | Ours You | You | Your | Yours They | Them | Their | Theirs [/tabla]

Comparatives and Superlatives

[tabla] SHORT ADJECTIVES (1-2) Adjective | Comparative | Superlative short | shorter than | the shortest big | bigger than | the biggest LONG ADJECTIVES (+2) Adjective | Comparative | Superlative interesting | more interesting than | the most interesting expensive | more expensive than | the most expensive [/tabla] [tabla] EXAMPLES Jean is taller than Charles. Scott is more responsible than Logan. Hank is the tallest person in this class. The X Mansion is the most impressive building in the country. [/tabla] [tabla] EXCEPTIONS Adjective | Comparative | Superlative good | better | the best bad | worse | the worst far | further / farther | the furthest / the farthest [/tabla]

a. Fill the gaps with the correct form of the verb To Be.

[fill_gaps] 1. He [is] (be) French. 2. We [aren't;are not] (not be) cold. 3. [Is] (be) he a footballer? 4. Where [is] (be) John? 5. We [are] (be) German. 6. He [isn't;is not] (not be) a journalist. 7. We [aren't;are not] (not be) singers. 8. [Are] (be) you a nurse? 9. Where [are] (be) David and Leo? 10. How [is] (be) your mum? 11. I [am;'m] (be) a professional guitarist. 12. I [am not;'m not] (not be) happy today. 13. [Am] (be) I your best friend? [/fill_gaps]

b. Fill the gaps with the Present Simple form of the following verbs.

[fill_gaps] 1. He [drives] (drive) to work everyday. 2. I [don't think;do not think] (not think) you're right. 3. [Do we have] (we/have) enough time? 4. I [eat] (eat) cereal in the morning. 5. [Does she travel] (she/travel) a lot? 6. You [watch] (watch) a lot of TV. 7. He [doesn't read;does not read] (not read) the newspaper. 8. [Does she dance] (she/dance) often? 9. What [do you do] (you/do) at the weekend? 10. Michael [doesn't drink;does not drink] (not drink) much tea. [/fill_gaps]

c. Fill the gaps with the Present Continuous form of the following verbs.

[fill_gaps] 1. She [is reading] (read) a book in her room. 2. They [aren't playing;are not playing] (not play) football right now. 3. [Is he cooking] (he/cook) dinner at the moment? 4. I [am studying;'m studying] (study) for my English exam. 5. We [aren't watching;are not watching] (not watch) TV this evening. 6. [Are you listening] (you/listen) to the teacher? 7. The children [are drawing] (draw) pictures in class. 8. He [isn't working;is not working] (not work) today because it's Sunday. 9. [Is she talking] (she/talk) to her best friend on the phone? 10. I [am walking;'m walking] (walk) to school right now. [/fill_gaps]

d. Fill the gaps with the correct Question Word.

[fill_gaps] 1. [why] are you here? You should be sleeping. 2. [when] do we have English class? Is it at 10 p.m.? 3. [how] did you get a 10 in the exam?! 4. [which] apple is tastier? This one or that one? 5. [what] are you doing? 6. [where;who] is John? [/fill_gaps]

e. Fill the gaps with the correct Comparative or Superlative form of the following adjectives.

[fill_gaps] 1. This car is [faster] (fast) than my old one. 2. Mount Everest is [the highest] (high) mountain in the world. 3. Today is [hotter] (hot) than yesterday. 4. That was [the worst] (bad) day of my life. 5. My house is [bigger] (big) than yours. 6. This restaurant is [the most expensive] (expensive) in town. 7. This book is [more interesting] (interesting) than the movie. 8. She is [the kindest] (kind) person I know. 9. She is [younger] (young) than her brother. 10. He is [the smartest] (smart) student in the class. [/fill_gaps]

item.title: 3rd ESO - LC1
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
Household tasks

[flashcards words="make your own breakfast:hacerte el desayuno, prepare food:preparar la comida, make your own lunch:hacerte la comida, shop for food:hacer la compra, clean the bathroom:limpiar el baño, load / unload the dishwasher:poner / quitar el lavavajillas, wash up:lavar los platos, take the rubbish out:sacar la basura, do the ironing:planchar, clean the windows:limpiar las ventanas, do the laundry:hacer la colada, make the bed:hacer la cama, tidy your room:ordernar tu habitación, vacuum the floor:aspirar"]

Qualities of good friends

[flashcards words="cheerful:alegre, confident:seguro de sí mismo, funny:divertido, generous:generoso, helpful:servicial, honest:sincero, kind:amable, loyal:leal, interesting:interesante"]

Free-time activities

[flashcards words="athletics:atletismo, bowling:bolos, chess:ajedrez, climbing:escalar, fitness training:entrenar en el gimnasio, gymnastics:gimnasia rítmica / deportiva, handball:handball, hiking:senderismo, hockey:hockey, judo:judo, karate:karate, surfing:surfear"]

Modal Verbs I
(permission, obligation & prohibition)

[flashcards words="can:poder,be allowed to:tener permiso para,must:deber,have to:tener que"] [tabla] STRUCTURE .A .b Affirmative /b /A | Subject + .A modal verb /A + base verb .R .b Negative /b /R | Subject + .R modal verb + not /R + base verb .V .b Interrogative /b /V | .V Modal verb /V + subject + base verb? [/tabla] [tabla] Can | Be allowed to | Must | Have to I .A can /A leave early | I' .A m allowed to /A leave early | You .A must /A wear a helmet | I .A have to /A work tomorrow I .R can't /R be here | I .R 'm not allowed to /R enter | You .R mustn't /R park here | I .R don't have to /R wake up early .V Can /V you smoke here? | ⚠️ .V Are /V you .V allowed to /V stay here? | | .V ⚠️ Do /V you .V have to /V leave now? [/tabla]

Modal Verbs II (ability and advice)

[flashcards words="can:saber (habilidad en el presente), could:saber (habilidad en el pasado), should:debería, be able to:saber (habilidad en el futuro)"] [tabla] STRUCTURE .A .b Affirmative /b /A | Subject + .A modal verb /A + base verb .R .b Negative /b /R | Subject + .R modal verb + not /R + base verb .V .b Interrogative /b /V | .V Modal verb /V + subject + base verb? [/tabla] [tabla] Can | Could | Should | Be able to I .A can /A swim | I .A could /A dance when I was a kid | You .A should /A rest | I .A will be able to /A watch this movie when I'm older I .R can't /R swim | I .R couldn't /R run back then | You .R shouldn't /R be rude | You .R won't be able to /R beat me in this game .V Can /V you swim? | .V Could /V you dance 5 years ago? | .V Sould /V we smoke here? | .V ⚠️ Will /V you .V be able to /V sleep with this noise? [/tabla]

Five people - Frankenstein, Wednesday, Astrid, Count Dracula and Jack Skellington - have written to the problem page of a magazine. Read them all and look up the vocabulary you don’t know.

Dear therapist,

I’ve got a problem and I need some advice.
My parents don’t like my friends. They blame them for everything that goes wrong. They say I have to stop going out with them. What should I do?

Wednesday Addams, 15

Hi,

I’m writing to ask you some advice with my problem. I never have any money and I’m always broke. As soon as I get my pocket money from Dr. Frankenstein, I spend it the first time I go out.

Frankenstein, 18

Hello,

I'm writing to you because I need advice. These days I´m very stressed with homework and tests. I feel like I don´t have time for anything, like meeting my friends!. I can´t have a quiet rest because the only thing that I think about is homework and studying.
What should I do?

Astrid Deetz, 16

Dear counselor,

Some people told me my brother has some new friends who are often in trouble with the police. Do you think I should tell my parents?

Jack Skellington, 16

Hello,

I moved here two months ago from Romania and still don’t know anyone apart from the people I work with. I don’t like going out during the day because the sunlight hurts me, so I find it very hard to meet people.

Count Dracula, 14

An email

You work on an advice website which helps teenagers with their problems. Here is the answer to Frankenstein’s mail. Choose one of the other five people’s mail (Wednesday Adams, Astrid Deetz, Jack Skelington or Count Dracula) and write an email giving them advice about what to do. Follow the structure below.

[tabla] STRUCTURE | EXAMPLE .b Greetings /b | .A Dear Frankenstein, /A .b Paragraph 1/b: say thanks for the email / express understanding of the problem. | .A Thank you for your mail. I’m sorry to hear that you’re having trouble. This is a problem a lot of people have. I know how hard it is to save money when there are so many cool things to spend it on. Here’s what I think you should do. /A .b Paragraph 2/b: formulate ideas to help that person with the problem. Use modal verbs (should, shouldn’t, ought to, have to… and connectors(so, because, firstly, also…). | .A Firstly, maybe you shouldn’t take all your money with you when you go out. You can try to take only what you need and leave the rest at home. Also, I think you should leave your credit card at home, because it is much easier to spend too much money when you use it. Another good thing is to keep an account of what you spend every day, so you can be aware of how much you are spending. /A .b Paragraph 3/b: conclusion. Express your support again by saying you hope your advice will be helpful. | .A You're not alone in this, and there are people who want to help you succeed. I hope this helps you solve your problem. /A .b Closure /b | .A Best wishes, /A .b Signature /b | .A (Your name) /A [/tabla] [tabla] Tips for each paragraph Paragraph 1 Thank you for your email. This is a very common problem. I’m sorry to hear that you’re having trouble. Paragraph 2 I think you should... In my opinion, you shouldn’t Why don’t you...? You have to... You can try... The best advice I can give you is... Paragraph 3 I hope this can help. Write me and let me know how things go. [/tabla]

Project Builder 1

Create a bubble chart to show how you spend your time.

You can follow this example:

(The less frequent activities)
(The relatively frequent activities)
(The most frequent activities)

Project Builder 2

Write 10 house rules using modal verbs for permission and obligation (can/can't, should/shouldn't, must/mustn't...). Include different areas of home life (housework, homework, TV, Internet, going out...).

Example: 1. Everybody must clean their dishes when we finish having lunch.

Project Builder 3

Write some sentences about how to manage online friendships. Use modal verbs to give advice (should, ought to). Here you have some ideas:

  • What kind of information should you share? With whom?
  • What should you do if you get a follow request from a stranger?
  • Should you engage in deep discussions? Why? Why not?

Project Builder 4

Give some advice to the people who need you! Read each person's problems and think of some pieces of advice for each one.

Final Project

Create a poster in Canva called 'My teenage survival guide'. Give advice on the problems teenagers have to face nowadays.

Sections:

  • HOMELIFE
  • FREE TIME
  • ONLINE FRIENDSHIPS

How to do it?

  • Write the title
  • Use Canva
  • Clear and appealing images
  • Include the information from your Project Builders!
  • Use MODAL VERBS
  • Only Keywords and images, NOT long explanations
  • Make it look attractive!
  • Speak for two (2) minutes

item.title: 2nd ESO: LC1 Extra Practice
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
a. Fill the gaps with the correct form of the Past Simple

[fill_gaps] 1. I [worked] (work) in a bank for ten years. 2. Where [did you live] (you/live) when you were young? 3. She [didn't study;did not study] (not study) French at university. 4. He [traveled] (travel) through the Middle East last year. 5. [Did they visit] (they/visit) the Louvre in Paris? 6. She [didn't watch;did not watch] (not watch) TV yesterday. 7. We [bought] (buy) a new car last weekend. 8. Where [did you teach] (you/teach) before you came here? 9. He [didn't think;did not think] (not think) that he was right. 10. I [cooked] (cook) steak last night. 11. [Did Lucy read] (Lucy/read) *War and Peace* at school? 12. She [didn't write;did not write] (not write) to her grandmother. 13. I [had] (have) a computer when I was a child. 14. Where [did you go] (you/go) on holiday? 15. I [loved] (love) ice cream when I was a child. 16. They [didn't meet;did not meet] (not meet) yesterday. 17. [Did she swim] (she/swim) in the sea in Greece? 18. We [didn't play;did not play] (not play) tennis yesterday because it was raining. 19. He [tried] (try) to lift the box but he couldn’t. 20. What [did you eat] (you/eat) for lunch? [/fill_gaps]

b. Fill the gaps with the correct form of There was / There were

[fill_gaps] 1. There [was] (be) a big storm last night. 2. There [were] (be) a lot of people at the concert. 3. There [wasn't;was not] (not be) any milk in the fridge. 4. [Was there] (be) a party at your house yesterday? 5. There [were] (be) many interesting books in the library. 6. There [weren't;were not] (not be) any buses after midnight. 7. [Were there] (be) any students in class this morning? 8. There [was] (be) a strange noise outside. 9. There [wasn't;was not] (not be) a cinema in our town years ago. 10. [Was there] (be) a problem with your computer? 11. There [were] (be) three dogs in the park. 12. There [weren't;were not] (not be) enough chairs for everyone. 13. [Were there] (be) any shops open on Sunday? 14. There [was] (be) a beautiful sunset yesterday evening. 15. There [wasn't;was not] (not be) time to finish the project. 16. There [were] (be) some old photos on the table. 17. [Was there] (be) a restaurant near the hotel? 18. There [weren't;were not] (not be) many tourists in winter. 19. There [was] (be) a good film on TV last night. 20. [Were there] (be) any tickets left for the concert? [/fill_gaps]

c. Fill the gaps with the Past Simple of the verb To Be

[fill_gaps] 1. I [was] (be) very tired after the trip. 2. You [weren't;were not] (not be) at school yesterday. 3. [Was he] (he/be) at the party last night? 4. They [were] (be) happy with their exam results. 5. She [wasn't;was not] (not be) in class this morning. 6. [Were you] (you/be) in London last summer? 7. We [were] (be) late for the meeting. 8. It [was] (be) really cold yesterday. 9. He [wasn't;was not] (not be) very friendly to me. 10. [Were they] (they/be) hungry after the match? 11. The movie [was] (be) fantastic! 12. The students [were] (be) very quiet during the test. 13. [Was it] (it/be) your birthday yesterday? 14. I [wasn't;was not] (not be) ready when you called. 15. We [were] (be) in the same team at school. 16. [Were you] (you/be) at home when the phone rang? 17. The weather [was] (be) perfect for a picnic. 18. She [wasn't;was not] (not be) sure about the answer. 19. [Were they] (they/be) on holiday in Spain? 20. My friends [were] (be) excited about the trip. [/fill_gaps]

item.title: 3rd ESO - LC2
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
Global issues

[flashcards words="animal rights:derechos de los animales, climate change:cambio climático, gender equality:igualdad de género, homelessness:sinhogarismo, pandemic:pandemia, pollution:contaminación, poverty:pobreza, racism:racismo"]

Contributions

[flashcards words="get involved:involucrarse, donate food:donar alimentos, pick up litter:recoger basura, use less plastic:utilizar menos plástico, make sure to recycle:asegurarse de reciclar, volunteer for:ofrecerse voluntari@ para"]

Compund nouns

[flashcards words="plastic packaging:embalaje de plástico, household rubbish:basura generada en el hogar, carbon footprint:huella de carbono, electric car:coche eléctrico, solar energy:energía solar, greenhouse gases:gases de efecto invernadero, fossil fuels:combustibles fósiles, recycling bin:papelera de reciclaje, endangered animal:animal en peligro de extinción, climate change:cambio climático"]

Future Tenses

[tabla] USE OF THE FUTURE TENSES Will | Be going to | Present Continuous | Present Simple .P 0. universal truths

1. spontaneous decisions

2. predictions without evidence /P | .P 1. plans

2. predictions with evidence /P | .P 1. future arrangements /P | .P 1. timetables /P [/tabla] [tabla] EXAMPLES 0. The sun will rise tomorrow.

1. I think I will go out.

2. I suppose the AI will replace humans in 2050. | 1. I'm going to visit my parents next week.

2. I can see dark clouds in the sky. It is going to rain. | 1. I'm meeting my friends at 19:00 | 1. English lessons start at 8:00 [/tabla]

First Conditional

  • Cause-consequence in the future
  • Possible and probable
[tabla] STRUCTURE .bIf/b|Present Simple|,|Simple Future (Will) [/tabla] [tabla] EXAMPLES .A Affirmative /A | .bIf/b I .Astudy/A a lot, I .Awill pass/A the exam. .R Negative /R | .bIf/b I .Rdon't study/R a lot, I .Rwon't/R pass the exam. .V Interrogative /V | .VWill/V I .Vpass/V the exam .bif/b I .Vstudy/V a lot? [/tabla] [tabla] INVERSIONS .bMain Structure/b | → | .bInversion/b .bIf/b I .Astudy/A a lot, I .Awill pass/A the exam. | → | I .Awill pass/A the exam .bif/b I .Astudy/A a lot. .bIf/b I .Rdon't study/R a lot, I .Rwon't/R pass the exam. | → | I .Rwon't/R pass the exam .bif/b I .Rdon't study/R a lot. .VWill/V I .Vpass/V the exam .bif/b I .Vstudy/V a lot? | → | .bIf/b I .Vstudy/V a lot, .Vwill/V I .Vpass/V the exam? [/tabla]

Second Conditional

[tabla] STRUCTURE .bIf/b|Past Simple|,|Would + Base Verb [/tabla] [tabla] EXAMPLES .A Affirmative /A | .bIf/b I .Astudied/A a lot, I .Awould pass/A the exam. .R Negative /R | .bIf/b I .Rdidn't study/R a lot, I .Rwouldn't/R pass the exam. .V Interrogative /V | .VWould/V I .Vpass/V the exam .bif/b I .Vstudied/V a lot? [/tabla] [tabla] INVERSIONS .bMain Structure/b | → | .bInversion/b .bIf/b I .Astudied/A a lot, I .Awould pass/A the exam. | → | I .Awould pass/A the exam .bif/b I .Astudied/A a lot. .bIf/b I .Rdidn't study/R a lot, I .Rwouldn't/R pass the exam. | → | I .Rwouldn't/R pass the exam .bif/b I .Rdidn't study/R a lot. .VWould/V I .Vpass/V the exam .bif/b I .Vstudied/V a lot? | → | .bIf/b I .Vstudied/V a lot, .Vwould/V I .Vpass/V the exam? [/tabla]

a. Use Future Tenses (Will, Be going to, Present Continuous, Present Simple)

[fill_gaps] 1. Look at those dark clouds! It [is going to rain] (rain). 2. I think people [will live] (live) on Mars one day. 3. Don’t worry, I [will help] (help) you with your bags. 4. We [are meeting] (meet) our teacher tomorrow afternoon. 5. The train [leaves] (leave) at 8:15 tomorrow morning. 6. She [won't come;will not come] (not come) to the party tonight. 7. There's somebody knocking outside, [will you open] (you/open) the door? 8. They [aren't having;are not having] (not have) dinner with us tonight. 9. What time [are you going] (you/go) to the party today? 10. The exam [starts] (start) at 9:00, so be on time. [/fill_gaps]

b. First Conditional: use the correct form of the verbs

[fill_gaps] 1. If it [rains] (rain), we [will stay] (stay) at home. 2. If you [study] (study) hard, you [will pass] (pass) the exam. 3. If she [doesn't hurry;does not hurry] (not hurry), she [will miss] (miss) the bus. 4. What [will you do] (you/do) if you [finish] (finish) early? 5. If they [don't wear;do not wear] (not wear) coats, they [will be] (be) cold. 6. If I [see] (see) him later, I [will tell] (tell) him the news. 7. If we [don't leave;do not leave] (not leave) now, we [won't arrive;will not arrive] (not arrive) on time. 8. [Will she be] (she/be) angry if you [forget] (forget) her birthday? 9. If you [eat] (eat) too much sugar, you [will feel] (feel) sick. 10. If he [doesn't practise;does not practise] (not practise) more, he [won't improve;will not improve] (not improve). [/fill_gaps]

c. Second Conditional: use the correct form of the verbs

[fill_gaps] 1. If I [had] (have) more time, I [would travel] (travel) around the world. 2. If she [studied] (study) harder, she [would get] (get) better marks. 3. If we [didn't live;did not live] (not live) here, we [would move] (move) to the city centre. 4. [Would you help] (you/help) me if I [asked] (ask) you? 5. I [would buy] (buy) that jacket if it [was] (be) cheaper. 6. If he [didn't forget;did not forget] (not forget) his homework, he [wouldn't get;would not get] (not get) into trouble. 7. We [wouldn't go;would not go] (not go) out if it [rained] (rain). 8. [Would they be] (they/be) happier if they [had] (have) more free time? 9. If you [were] (be) me, what [would you do] (you/do)? 10. She [would pass] (pass) the exam if she [worked] (work) harder. [/fill_gaps]

item.title: Definitions
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
[fancy-button] text= ← 2nd ESO link= https://englishandchill.com/2nd-eso/ [/fancy-button]
[fancy-button] text= ← 3rd ESO link= https://englishandchill.com/3rd-eso/ [/fancy-button]

How to write a proper definition

[tabla] 1. Identify the word type .b Types /b | .b Examples /b · object / idea (.Vthat/which/V) | 'pencil' / 'love' · place (.Vwhere/V) | 'cinema' · moment (.Vwhen/V) | 'Christmas' · people (.Vwho/V) | 'police officer' 2. Use this structure .b Types /b | .b Examples /b objects / ideas | A .A pencil /A is an object .V that /V we use to write something. places | A .A cinema /A is the place .V where /V we can watch films. moment | .A Christmas /A is the time of the year .V when /V we get presents and sing carols. people | A .A police officer /A is a person .V who /V fights crime and protects us from criminals. [/tabla]

Practice

Try defining these words, then flip the cards and see how you did:

[flashcards words="gym:It's a place where you can exercise., wizard:It's a person who makes magic., dragon:It's a feroucious creature that spits fire., holidays:It's the time when we can rest from work or school."]

item.title: 3rd ESO - LC4
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
Film Genres

[flashcards words="action film:película de acción, animation:animación, comedy:comedia, fantasy film:película de fantasía, historical drama:drama histórico, horror film:película de terror, musical:musical, romance:romance, science fiction film:película de ciencia ficción, thriller:suspense / thriller, war film:película bélica, western:película del oeste / western"]

Filmmaking

[flashcards words="camera:cámara, close-up:primer plano, director:director/a, lines:diálogos, prop:utilería / atrezo, script:guion, special effects:efectos especiales, cut:cortar, edit:editar, rehearse:ensayar, shoot:rodar / filmar"]

Storytelling

[flashcards words="performance:actuación / interpretación, Academy Awards:Premios de la Academia (Oscars), character:personaje, play the part:interpretar el rol, star:protagonizar, audience:audiencia, special effects:efectos especiales, based on:basado en, plot:argumento / trama, critic:crítico/a, set in:ambientado en, series:serie"]

Extreme Adjectives

[flashcards words="furious:furioso (very angry), huge:enorme (very big), freezing:helado (very cold), terrified: aterrado (very scared), hilarious:hilarante (very funny), starving:famélico (very hungry), fascinating:fascinante (very interesting), ancient:antiguo (very old), terrifying:aterrador (very scary), tiny:enano (very small), exhausted:agotado (very tired)"]

Past Simple & Past Continuous

[tabla] PAST SIMPLE & PAST CONTINUOUS Main Structure .b.YPC/Y/b+ .Lwhen/L + .b.APS/A/b|I .Ywas driving/Y .Lwhen/L my phone .Arang/A. .b.APS/A/b + .Lwhile/L + .b.YPC/Y/b|I .Nfell/N .Lwhile/L I .Ywas dancing/Y [/tabla] [tabla] Inversions & Variations .Lwhen/L + .b.APS/A/b, .b.YPC/Y/b|.LWhen/L my phone .Arang/A, I .Ywas driving/Y. .Lwhile/L + .b.YPC/Y/b, .b.APS/A/b|.LWhile/L I .Ywas dancing/Y, I .Afell/A. .Lwhile/L + .b.YPC/Y/b, .b.YPC/Y/b|.LWhile/L I .Ywas studying/Y, my sister .Ywas watching/Y TV.Y/Y. [/tabla]

Too/Too much/Too many/(not) Enough

[tabla] TOO/TOO MUCH/TOO MANY/(NOT) ENOUGH Form | Use | Example .Ptoo/P + adj | More than wanted (degree) | It's .Ptoo/P cold outside. .Ytoo much/Y + noun | More than wanted (uncountable) | She eats .Ytoo much/Y sugar. .Vtoo many/V + noun | More than wanted (countable) | There are .Vtoo many/V people. .Aenough/A + noun | The right amount | We have .Aenough/A time. .Rnot enough/R + noun | Less than needed | There isn't .Renough/R bread. adj + .Aenough/A | Sufficient degree | She's old .Aenough/A to vote. not + adj + .Renough/R | Insufficient degree | I am not tall .Renough/R to be a model. [/tabla]

a. Choose the correct form of Past Continuous or Past Simple

[fill_gaps] 1. Helen [was sleeping] (sleep) when I came home. 2. I [wasn't watching;was not watching] (not watch) TV when the phone rang. 3. [Did the vase break] (the vase/break) while you were cleaning the shelf? 4. She [was reading] (read) a book when her friend called. 5. We [weren't playing;were not playing] (not play) in the park when it started raining. 6. I [was cooking] (cook) dinner when my brother arrived. 7. [Was he talking] (he/talk) on the phone when you knocked on the door? 8. They [arrived] (arrive) while we were having lunch. 9. My sister [wasn't sleeping;was not sleeping] (not sleep) when the alarm went off. 10. [Were you reading] (you/read) a magazine when the lights went out? [/fill_gaps]

b. Choose the correct form of Past Continuous or Past Simple

[fill_gaps] 1. While I [was studying] (study), the electricity [went] (go) out. 2. The students [were talking] (talk) while the teacher [was writing] (write) on the board. 3. When the fire alarm [rang] (ring), we [were having] (have) a meeting. 4. While they [were waiting] (wait) for the bus, it [started] (start) to snow. 5. I [wasn't listening;was not listening] (not listen) when the boss [gave] (give) the instructions. 6. While my mom [was driving] (drive), she [saw] (see) a beautiful rainbow. 7. [Was it raining] (it / rain) while you [were walking] (walk) to the gym? 8. We [found] (find) some old photos while we [were tidying] (tidy) the attic. 9. When you [called] (call) me, I [wasn't doing;was not doing] (not do) anything important. 10. While the chef [was preparing] (prepare) the soup, the assistants [were chopping] (chop) the vegetables. [/fill_gaps]

c. Fill the blanks with Too, Too much, Too many, or (not) Enough

[fill_gaps] 1. I can't drink this coffee. It has [too much] sugar. 2. We can't go for a walk. It is [too] windy outside. 3. I'm sorry, I don't have [enough] money to buy that car. 4. There are [too many] cars in this city; the traffic is terrible! 5. Is he tall [enough] to reach the top shelf? 6. You shouldn't watch [too much] TV; it's bad for your eyes. 7. I didn't win the race because I wasn't fast [enough]. 8. There are [not enough] chairs in the room. Some people have to stand up. 9. The shoes were [too] expensive, so I didn't buy them. 10. We have [enough] food for the party, so don't buy anything else. [/fill_gaps]

d. Write the complete sentences placing Too, Too much, Too many, or (not) Enough in the correct position

[fill_gaps] 1. small / shirt / is / for / me / too / this [This shirt is too small for me] 2. soup / salt / much / in / too / there / is / the [There is too much salt in the soup;There's too much salt in the soup] 3. loud / music / is / the / enough / ? [Is the music loud enough?] 4. many / there / mistakes / were / too / the / in / essay [There were too many mistakes in the essay] 5. sleep / last / get / enough / didn't / night / I [I didn't get enough sleep last night;I did not get enough sleep last night] 6. to / lift / box / strong / enough / isn't / he / that [He isn't strong enough to lift that box;He is not strong enough to lift that box] 7. people / at / many / there / the / were / concert / too [There were too many people at the concert] 8. water / to / swim / warm / the / enough / is / ? [Is the water warm enough to swim?] 9. time / finish / we / have / don't / enough / project / the / to [We don't have enough time to finish the project;We do not have enough time to finish the project] 10. dark / to / it / see / too / is / anything [It is too dark to see anything;It's too dark to see anything] [/fill_gaps]

item.title: 2nd ESO: LC4
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
Spaces

[flashcards words="bench:banco,bridge:puente,café:cafetería,lake:lago,open-air theatre:teatro al aire libre,outdoor gym:gimnasio al aire libre,path:camino,picnic area:zona de picnic,playground:patio,skatepark:skatepark"]

Verbs (good deeds)

[flashcards words="donate:donar,help:ayudar,offer:ofrecer / ofrecerse a,put:poner / depositar,volunteer:ofrecerse voluntari@"]

Phrasal verbs

[flashcards words="find out:descubrir,pick up:recoger,turn on:encender,go away:irse / alejarse,take part in:formar parte de,work out:entrenar,look after:cuidar de,talk over:hablar sobre,look at:mirar a,turn off:apagar"]

bench · bridge · café · lake · open-air theatre · outdoor gym · path · picnic area · playground · skatepark · donate · help · volunteer · turn off · find out

[fill_gaps] 1. We can sit on the [bench] to rest for a while. 2. Let’s cross the river using the [bridge]. 3. I usually drink hot chocolate in the [café]. 4. The [lake] is very big and has ducks swimming in it. 5. Tonight we are going to the [open-air theatre] to watch a play. 6. My dad exercises every morning in the [outdoor gym]. 7. This [path] goes to the forest. 8. Let’s have lunch together in the [picnic area]. 9. The children are playing football in the [playground]. 10. Teenagers like to skate in the [skatepark]. 11. You can [donate] your old clothes to charity. 12. I want to [help] my grandparents with the shopping. 13. She decided to [volunteer] her time to the community. 14. Please [turn off] the TV before going to bed. 15. We are going to [find out] the new museum tomorrow. [/fill_gaps] [tabla] WILL / BE GOING TO WILL|BE GOING TO Universal truths|- Spontaneous decisions|Plans Predictions without evidence|Predictions with evidence [/tabla] [tabla] EXAMPLES WILL|BE GOING TO The sun .bwill rise/b tomorrow at 8:00.|- I promise I .bwill help/b you!|.bI'm going to travel/b to Egypt next month. I think it .bwill rain/b tomorrow.|Look at those dark clouds! It .bis going to rain/b. [/tabla]

a. Use the correct form of 'will'.

[fill_gaps] 1. I [will call;'ll call] (call) you later. 2. She [won't go;will not go] (not go) to the party. 3. [Will they arrive] (they/arrive) on time? 4. He [will help;'ll help] (help) you with your homework. 5. We [won't be;will not be] (not be) there tomorrow. 6. [Will you visit] (you/visit) your grandparents this weekend? 7. They [will start;'ll start] (start) the meeting at 10 a.m. 8. [Will it rain] (it/rain) today? 9. I [won't forget;will not forget] (not forget) your birthday. 10. [Will she cook] (she/cook) dinner tonight? [/fill_gaps]

b. Use the correct form of 'be going to'

[fill_gaps] 1. I [am going to study; I'm going to study] (study) for the exam tomorrow. 2. She [is going to visit; she's going to visit] (visit) her grandparents next weekend. 3. They [are not going to join; aren't going to join] (not join) us for dinner. 4. [Are you going to watch] (you/watch) the movie tonight? 5. We [are going to travel; 're going to travel] (travel) to Paris next month. 6. He [is not going to participate; isn't going to participate] (not participate) in the competition. 7. [Is she going to help] (she/help) you with your homework? 8. I [am going to start; I'm going to start] (start) a new project at work. 9. We [are going to buy; 're going to buy] (buy) a new car next year. 10. [Are they going to attend] (they/attend) the conference? [/fill_gaps]

c. Use the correct form of 'will' or 'be going to'

[fill_gaps] 1. I'm not really sure, but I think it [will rain] (rain) today. 2. My best friend Ella told me that she [is going to travel] (travel) to France next summer. 3. Hi! Do you need help? I ['ll do; will do] (do) it! 4. We [are going to cook] (cook) dinner together tonight at 20:00. 5. Excuse me, [will you help] (you/help) me cross the street? I'm a little bit old. 6. He can't come to the party because he [is going to watch] (watch) a movie with his sister. 7. It's already June... It [is going to be] (be) really hot soon. 8. I may be wrong, but I believe robots [will rule] (rule) the world in 4050. 9. I have a feeling that today [will be] (be) a good day. 10. They [are going to buy] (buy) a new car soon. John told me this morning. [/fill_gaps]

Project Builder 1

There is an empty, abandoned lot in your neighbourhood and the mayor wants to transform it. Town hall is accepting proposals to turn it into a useful space for the community.

Choose 3 of the following problems in your community to address your proposal and write 3 solutions (one for each) in your notebook:

  • Teenagers don’t have a place to hang
    out.
  • There are many stray cats.
  • The area is very dirty and full of litter.
  • The neighbors don’t know each other.
  • Elderly people feel lonely.
  • There are no places to eat outdoors.
  • The neighbors don’t do a lot of exercise.
  • The parents in the neighborhood want
    activities and spaces for their kids.
  • Dog owners don’t know where to walk
    their dogs.
  • There is no library in the neighborhood.
  • The neighborhood is very boring and
    gray.
  • Disabled people don’t have adapted
    spaces to enjoy their free time.

Project Builder 2

Design 3 park features and each one has to include a justification about why your design is the best of all. Remember that all the features you choose for your park need to have a justification to benefit the whole community.

Project Builder 3

Write 2 pros and 1 con for each of your solutions.

Final Project: Writing

Write a text about your proposals individually.​ You can use Word Reference and your notebook ONLY.​ You have to turn it in at the end of the class.

Don't forget to:

  • Include a title​
  • Use connectors​
  • Use the LC’s vocabulary​
  • Use will and be going to​
  • Include the relevant information from your Project Builders
[tabla] STRUCTURE .bParagraph 1/b|Mention the city, some of the city’s problems and which ones you are going to address. .bParagraph 2/b|Explain the 3 solutions in your proposal and 2 pros and 1 con of any solution (you choose). .bParagraph 3/b|Closure: explain why your proposals will help improve the neighbourhood. [/tabla]
