item.title: 3rd ESO - LC5
item.dc:creator: adri
item.content:encoded:
Present Perfect

[tabla] Main Structure .A.bAffirmative/A/b | I .Ahave worked/A here .Lfor/L 4 years. .b.RNegative/R/b | She .Rhasn't seen/R that movie .Lsince/L she was a baby. .b.VQuestion/V/b | .VHave/V you .Vbeen/V to Paris? [/tabla] [tabla] Keywords Keyword|Used for|Examples .Lfor/L | .Lperiods of time/L | five years
ten months
four hours
a lot of time
a while
ages .Lsince/L | .Ldates/L | 1995
I was a kid
you left university
my childhood
last week
my first birthday .Lever/L | .L'alguna vez'/L | .VHave/V you .Lever/L .Vwatched/V this movie?
.VHas/V she .Lever/L .Vvisited/V New York? .Lnever/L | .L'nunca'/L | We .Ahave/A .Lnever/L .Adone/A this before.
My brother .Ahas/A .Lnever/L .Abroken/A any glass at home. [/tabla]

Present Perfect vs. Past Simple

[tabla] Present Perfect|Past Simple (for/since/ever/never)
we don't mention when it happened|(yesterday, last week, this morning...)
we mention when it happened Jane .Ahas won/A many awards as an actress.|Jane .Avolunteered/A for Green Peace .Lin 2010/L. Alfred .Ahas looked after/A Bruce .Lsince/L he was 11.|Bruce .Alost/A his parents .Lwhen he was only a child/L. I .Ahave/A .Lnever/L .Adesigned/A a website before.|I .Adesigned/A a website .Llast year/L at work. .VHave/V you .Lever/L .Vwondered/V what you would do if your house was set on fire?|.LLast night/L I .Awondered/A what I would do if my house was set on fire. [/tabla]

a. Use the correct form of the Present Perfect

[fill_gaps] 1. Helen [has lived] (live) in Paris since 2020. 2. I [haven't seen;have not seen] (not see) my uncle for five years. 3. [Have you known] (you/know) your best friend since childhood? 4. She [has worked] (work) at this company for six months. 5. We [haven't played;have not played] (not play) tennis since last summer. 6. I [have had] (have) this car for ages. 7. [Has he studied] (he/study) English since he was a child? 8. They [have been] (be) married for ten years. 9. My sister [hasn't felt;has not felt] (not feel) well since yesterday. 10. [Have they lived] (they/live) in Spain for a long time? [/fill_gaps]

b. Fill the gaps with 'for' or 'since'

[fill_gaps] 1. Helen has lived in Paris [since] 2020. 2. I haven't seen my uncle [for] five years. 3. Have you known your best friend [since] childhood? 4. She has worked at this company [for] six months. 5. We haven't played tennis [since] last summer. 6. I have had this car [for] ages. 7. Has he studied English [since] he was a child? 8. They have been married [since] COVID-19. 9. My sister hasn't felt well [since] yesterday. 10. Have they lived in Spain [for] a long time? [/fill_gaps]

c. Use 'ever' or 'never'

[fill_gaps] 1. My friends have [never] seen a dolphin in the ocean. 2. Have you [ever] won a medal at school? 3. She has [never] eaten broccoli because she doesn't like vegetables. 4. This is the biggest museum I have [ever] visited. 5. Has your brother [ever] ridden a horse? 6. We have [never] missed the school bus this year. 7. Have they [ever] played a video game together? 8. My teacher has [never] shouted in class. 9. It is the most beautiful drawing you have [ever] made. 10. I have [never] broken a bone playing football. [/fill_gaps]

d. Use Present Perfect or Past Simple

[fill_gaps] 1. I [have lost] (lose) my favourite pen. I can't find it anywhere! 2. Last summer, we [travelled;traveled] (travel) to the beach with my family. 3. [Have you ever seen] (you/ever/see) a real elephant? 4. My brother [didn't play;did not play] (not play) video games yesterday afternoon. 5. Look! Lucas [has broken] (break) his toy car. He is crying. 6. They [moved] (move) to this city three years ago. 7. She [has finished] (finish) her homework, so she can watch TV. 8. We [didn't go;did not go] (not go) to school last Monday because it was a holiday. 9. [Did you watch] (you/watch) the football match on TV last night? 10. My parents [have lived] (live) in this house since I was born. [/fill_gaps]
